title: Profiling, Event-Loop Health, Memory, Load, and Capacity · Contract Lens: Load Model Percentiles
summary: A team optimizes microbenchmarks, measures only average latency, ignores coordinated omission and warmup, raises concurrency until throughput peaks, and has no resource or cost model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts load model percentiles increment in performance experiment and capacity envelope. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model arrival rate, concurrency, payload, endpoint mix, warmup, caches, connection reuse, percentiles, coordinated omission, errors, and saturation.
Use CPU profiles, heap snapshots, allocation, garbage collection, event-loop delay, handles, and request traces to locate causal bottlenecks safely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Load Model Percentiles · Theory Load Model Percentiles
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7170 in a recovery drill following an uncertain transaction commit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate tail latency with event-loop and utilization capacity evidence; show recovery after the overload input is removed; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model arrival rate, concurrency, payload, endpoint mix, warmup, caches, connection reuse, percentiles, coordinated omission, errors, and saturation.
